title: Secrets, Dependencies, Static Analysis, and Supply Chain · Mastery Check
summary: Build layered security gates for source, dependencies, workflows, and releases. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a secure-development gate stack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prevent, detect, revoke, and remediate committed credentials and sensitive files.
Scan direct and transitive dependencies and triage exploitability and reachability.
Use static security analysis for data flow and dangerous patterns with reviewed queries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Secret Scanning · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates security. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Deleting a secret from the latest commit removes its exposure. This fails the Secret Scanning contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Artifact Provenance applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record build provenance, checksums, signatures, and dependency inventories when risk requires.
